name: Madeiran Portuguese
notes: Madeiran Portuguese (Ribeira Brava, Funchal). Categorical features per DIALECT_PATTERNS / whitepaper5 (TigreGotico internal dialect research): (1) NASAL DIPHTHONG → NASAL+N — the major productive pattern: plural endings -ões→[õns], -ães→[ɐ̃ns] (verões→[vɨˈɾõns], cães→[kɐ̃ns]); encoded as multi-character graphemes; (2) /v/ ≠ /b/ — distinction preserved, no betacism; (3) nasal vowels retain broad [ɐ̃] quality. NOTES-ONLY (sporadic/lexical — not encoded): l-palatalization (quilo→quilho, mochila→mochilha — specific lexical items only; retained as allophone variant); r-diphthongization (correr→[kuˈʁweɾ] — lexical, not systematic); Filipa→[fʎipɐ] — a lexicalized item. NOTES-ONLY: stop aspiration [pʰ, tʰ, kʰ] and final devoicing of voiced obstruents are suprasegmental/allophonic features outside grapheme schema scope. Sources: DIALECT_PATTERNS.md §Nasal Patterns §Palatalization; whitepaper5 §5.

ancestors.notes: Standard European Portuguese; Madeiran diverges in nasal diphthong reduction (→nasal+n), and retains archaic vowel qualities from 15th-century settlement

sources.author: Boléo, M.P.
sources.title: Estudos de linguística portuguesa e românica

sources.author: TigreGotico
sources.title: DIALECT_PATTERNS.md and whitepaper5: Phoneme-Level Dialect Transforms for European Portuguese
sources.notes: Internal dialect feature matrix and IPA transform inventory; pending external peer review
